title: Xerisimo Quick-Fix Spring Pao de Queijo
description: A quick-fix Brazilian dish featuring a yeasty, springy puff pastry crust infused with Ras el hanout, perfect for a sugar-free-friendly breakfast or snack. Use a bamboo tray to achieve the ideal crispy crunch.
instructions: Dust a bamboo tray with a thin layer of flour, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
In a small bowl, combine yeast and warmed milk. Let it sit for 5 minutes until frothy.
In a stand mixer fitted with a dough hook attachment, combine flour, Ras el hanout, salt, and sugar. Mix for 2 minutes until combined.
Add melted butter and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until smooth.
Roll out the dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch.
Place the dough in the prepared bamboo tray and brush the edges with the beaten egg.
Place the thawed puff pastry on top of the dough, pressing the edges to seal.
Cut a few slits in the top of the pastry to allow steam to escape.
Brush the top with the beaten egg wash and sprinkle with sugar-free granulated sugar, if desired.
Bake for 25-30 minutes or until golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 5 minutes before serving
